slices.title: Workspace tree scanner
slices.rollout: persistent-session canary with one-shot and Node rollback
slices.sidecarCommand: vibelink workspace-tree-sidecar (persistent) or vibelink workspace-tree (one-shot fallback)
slices.nodeFallback: A failed persistent session falls back to the one-shot Rust CLI; src/workspaces.js listDirectory() is used when Rust is off, missing, invalid, truncated, or both Rust routes fail. Auto mode skips a missing binary without recording failure.
slices.runtimeStats: rustWorkspaceTree stats expose mode, availability, route/cache/budget outcomes and nested session mode, active/ready state, starts, failures, fallbacks, last error, pending, request/response, timeout, backpressure, and termination counters.
slices.currentState: Rust one-shot CLI and persistent JSONL sidecar, Node opt-in/auto routing, sidecar-to-one-shot-to-Node fallback, bounded pending requests, runtime session stats, root routing, supported-subset parity, inherited/nested gitignore rules, Windows metadata parity, Node-compatible locale/BFS ordering, conservative fallback for truncated Rust subsets, signature/cache behavior, content-safe file caching, contract/client/integration tests, three-repository canaries, and an independent Windows CI gate exist. Persistent runs preserved exact parity, full cache reuse, one sidecar start, clean pending drain/termination, and zero route/session failures or fallbacks. VibeLink measured 30.0-31.0ms Node vs 53.7-80.3ms Rust cold; ok-wuthering-waves 77.2ms vs 59.8ms; meetily 76.1ms vs 77.1ms. Warm totals were 9.2-26.0ms.
slices.nextAction: Keep the persistent sidecar in limited canary while remote CI and interactive sessions confirm readiness, clean drain, and zero session fallback; then consider default-on with the one-shot and Node rollback paths retained.
slices.promotionGate: Can move to default-on only after CI and limited real/interactive sessions preserve exact supported-subset parity, one ready persistent sidecar, zero route/session failures and fallbacks, bounded pending drain, clean termination, warm cache reuse, nested gitignore invalidation, acceptable cold/warm latency, and tested session-to-one-shot-to-Node rollback.

slices.title: Persistent MCP stdio sessions
slices.rollout: auto-mode canary with manual rollback flag
slices.nodeFallback: src/mcpRuntime.js falls back to the existing Node stdio probe/call path and closes the failed Rust sidecar client on readiness, probe, or call failure. VIBELINK_MCP_RUST_SIDECAR=auto skips a missing command without marking a Rust failure.
slices.runtimeStats: getMcpRustSidecarStats() exposes enabled, mode, auto/available/active/ready/failed state, command, args, starts, failures, fallbacks, last failure, last error, and client stats.
slices.currentState: Node manager, JSONL client, real Rust mcp-session-sidecar, auto readiness routing, contract/burst/timeout/crash/backpressure tests, synthetic/generic real-session/multi-session soak harnesses, packaged-command resolution, and an independent scheduled Windows CI gate exist. The Rust launcher supplies its current executable as the default MCP/event/workspace command while preserving explicit overrides. The synthetic canary reduced MCP server spawns from 13 to 1. A five-session soak reduced 65 baseline MCP spawns to 5, with 5/5 clean sessions, zero failures/fallbacks/backpressure/pending, and 170.6ms maximum request latency. Real codebase-memory and Headroom sessions also passed with one sidecar and clean drain.
slices.nextAction: Keep the weekly Windows soak green and collect representative production auto-mode evidence before considering default-on. The second real implementation, multi-session stability, packaged-command resolution, and explicit rollback gates are satisfied.
slices.promotionGate: Can move to default-on only after CI and limited real MCP sessions preserve zero readiness fallback after command availability, zero client and sidecar backpressure under normal load, material spawn reduction, cached tools/list behavior, clean pending drain, and tested fallback without task-visible failure. Rollback with VIBELINK_MCP_RUST_SIDECAR=0 must remain documented and tested.

slices.title: Event store append/replay sidecar
slices.rollout: auto readiness canary with manual rollback flag
slices.nodeFallback: src/db.js prefers the Rust sidecar when VIBELINK_EVENT_STORE_RUST_SIDECAR=1, or when VIBELINK_EVENT_STORE_RUST_SIDECAR=auto and the command exists. Missing binary in manual mode, bad health, timeout, invalid JSON, request failure, or sidecar exit falls back to the Node Worker when enabled, otherwise sync SQLite. Missing binary in auto mode skips Rust without marking failure.
slices.runtimeStats: getEventStoreRuntimeStats() in src/db.js exposes mode, rustSidecar enabled/auto/available/active/ready/failed, starts/failures/fallbacks/lastError/client stats, worker status, batch append stats, and eventStore metrics.
slices.currentState: Node Worker boundary, event-store metrics, batchers, JSONL sidecar client, shared method allowlist, real Rust event-store-sidecar command, read-only/query-only mode, contract and write-rejection tests, explicit opt-in runtime routing, auto readiness, Worker/sync fallback tests, rollback docs, robust canary thresholds, local/runtime/server/real-data harnesses, and CI status wiring exist. A 2026-07-11 canary read the approximately 1.01GB existing database and preserved exact task/tool/live list, unified, and replay-window parity across 9 comparisons with zero failures/pending. Rust batch append avoids repeated owner lookups and uses last_insert_rowid on the normal insert hot path.
slices.nextAction: Run limited human-driven real-session canaries with runtime stats capture before broader default exposure.
slices.promotionGate: Can move to default-on only after canary metrics remain within documented thresholds across automated and human-driven representative runtime sessions, CI/status checks cover contract/runtime/canary verification, and rollback remains tested and documented.

slices.title: Live audio low-latency pipeline
slices.nodeFallback: Existing live-call audio and ASR paths remain Node/provider-owned because no production router exists. A future opt-in client must fall back on missing command, bad health, timeout, invalid JSON, request failure, or exit.
slices.runtimeStats: Contract-side stats expose processed/dropped/buffered/evicted chunks, samples, and bytes; sequence gaps/duplicates/out-of-order; backpressure, request/response/failure, pending, timestamps, limits, and last error. Production routing stats do not exist yet.
slices.currentState: Protocol v1, shared JavaScript constants, an independent Node fixture, a real Rust audio-pipeline-sidecar, deterministic PCM16 level/peak/RMS and silence behavior, safe sequence accounting, bounded ring eviction/backpressure counters, structured validation errors, Rust unit tests, cross-language contract tests, a representative PCM benchmark, and an independent Windows CI gate exist. A 2026-07-11 run measured Node RMS p95 at 0.003-0.008ms and persistent Rust JSONL p95 at 0.189-0.356ms for 10/20/100ms frames with zero numeric delta, drops, or backpressure. No production client or live-call/ASR routing was changed.
slices.nextAction: Keep RMS-only processing contract-only because current measurements show no material Node bottleneck or Rust speedup. After concurrent live-call work is reconciled, re-run the benchmark only for a materially different bounded workload such as VAD/resampling before considering an optional client. Keep ASR/provider behavior out of this slice.
slices.promotionGate: Can move to opt-in only after concurrent live-call changes are reconciled, representative measurements show a material Node bottleneck or Rust benefit for the proposed workload, an optional bounded client exists behind VIBELINK_AUDIO_RUST_PIPELINE=1, all missing/bad/timeout/invalid/exit paths fall back to current Node behavior, and runtime metrics plus real PCM canaries remain healthy.

slices.title: Compression and context budget helper
slices.nodeEntry: src/compressionContract.js defines protocol constants for tests; no production router exists
slices.nodeFallback: Existing src/compactService.js and src/contextBudget.js behavior remains authoritative because no production path routes to this contract-only sidecar.
slices.runtimeStats: The Rust stats method exposes requests, responses, failures, bytesIn, bytesOut, timestamps, pending, and lastError.
slices.currentState: Protocol v1, shared JavaScript constants, an independent Node fixture, a real Rust compression-sidecar command, UTF-8-safe head/tail byte trimming, overlap-safe head/tail log sampling, structured errors, runtime stats, Rust unit tests, cross-language contract tests, a reproducible Node hot-path benchmark, and an independent Windows CI contract gate exist. Two 2026-07-11 runs measured the existing Node production functions at 0.253-0.353ms combined p95 on the largest real task stream and 0.425-0.547ms p95 on a 1000-event/2,000,000-character synthetic upper bound, below the 10ms material-bottleneck threshold. There is intentionally no production routing, tokenizer claim, or semantic summarization.
slices.nextAction: Keep this slice contract-only because current measurements do not justify another process boundary. Re-run the benchmark if runtime telemetry or workload shape changes materially; add an optional fallback-capable client only if contrary evidence exceeds the documented threshold.
slices.promotionGate: Can move to opt-in only after measured need is documented, an optional production client exists behind VIBELINK_RUST_COMPRESSION=1, missing/failed/invalid/timeout sidecars preserve current Node behavior, runtime stats expose routing outcomes, and docs continue to state this is not semantic summarization or provider-token precision.
